summary: EditCol V3 — iteration 3 retest of the two iteration-2 frontend fixes. Both fixes verified PASS. (1) Register Step 1 role-tab grammar now reads 'I'm a client' and 'I'm an editor' (correct article). (2) BecomeEditor.jsx redirect logic moved into useEffect — all 4 redirect scenarios now work reliably and well within the SLA: (a) not-logged-in click on hero-join-btn lands on /register?role=editor with editor tab pre-selected; (b) logged-in client visiting /become-editor renders the upgrade-to-editor button (no infinite Loading state) and clicking it redirects to /editor/onboarding in ~0.20s (well under 3s); (c) already-editor visiting /become-editor auto-redirects to /editor/onboarding in ~0.55s (under 2s); (d) admin visiting /become-editor auto-redirects in ~0.26s. Backend not retested per request (was 100% green in iteration 2). Note: an initial false-negative occurred because the test re-used a hardcoded phone number 5551234567 that was already registered by previous iterations — once a unique phone was used, all flows passed.
critical_code_review_comments: BecomeEditor.jsx fix is clean — useEffect with [ready, user, nav] deps; intermediate Loading state correctly covers the case ready=false OR user undefined OR user.role in {editor,admin} (the latter is shown only for the single render before the effect fires). One nit: the loading guard condition `(user && (user.role === 'editor' || user.role === 'admin'))` causes a transient Loading flash for editor/admin users before the effect redirect runs — this is acceptable (<1 frame) but could be avoided by inverting the check to render the upgrade UI only when user.role === 'client'. Functional behavior is correct.
Register.jsx: role tab article now computed inline `r === 'editor' ? 'an' : 'a'` — fine for the 2-role case; if more roles are added (e.g. 'agency') consider a small helper.

Pre-existing (carryover from iter 2): /api/applications/{id}/decline does not check application status; AI-match deterministic fallback 'reason' copy can be misleading when there are no badges and price>budget; brute-force lockout test is flaky behind K8s ingress (rate-limit by email+ip where ip rotates). Not regressions.
test_credentials: Admin financeabuzar@gmail.com / EditColAdmin@2026!Secure (verified working). For client retest a fresh user was registered via /register: v3rt_7742428@example.com / TestPass123! (phone 9283677743). IMPORTANT for future iterations: use a UNIQUE random phone number per test run; the backend correctly rejects duplicates with a 400 'Phone already registered'.
seed_data_creation: 1 new client user v3rt_7742428@example.com created and upgraded to editor during the test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both iteration-2 frontend issues are resolved. Hero-join-btn → unauthenticated goes to /register?role=editor; logged-in client lands on /become-editor → upgrade button visible → click → /editor/onboarding in <1s; editor/admin auto-redirect from /become-editor to onboarding in <1s. Register role tabs now read 'I'm a client' and 'I'm an editor'. When writing fresh-registration tests, always generate a unique random phone (e.g., '9' + 9 random digits) — reusing a hardcoded phone fails with 400 once any prior test has used it.
rca of the issue: RCA of the iteration-2 redirect bug (now fixed): BecomeEditor.jsx previously called nav() directly during render for the not-logged-in and already-editor branches. This is a React anti-pattern: under StrictMode the render may run twice, the second render's navigation can race with the AuthContext refresh that triggered the re-render, and React Router may cancel one of the navigations. Fix applied by main agent (lines 22-30): redirect logic moved into useEffect([ready, user, nav]) and the component returns a non-empty 'Loading…' placeholder for any not-yet-final auth state; the upgrade() event handler (which already worked) is unchanged. Verified by browser automation: 4 of 4 scenarios redirect within their SLA windows (0.20s, 0.55s, 0.26s for the upgrade/editor/admin paths).
